D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

Gold Nanoparticles — Plasmonic Resonance

bulk gold is yellow-metallic; suspend 20-nm gold particles in water and the solution is ruby red — collective electron oscillation absorbs green light at the plasmon resonance

The Lycurgus Cup (Roman, 4th century) is dichroic — green by reflection, red by transmission — because of suspended gold nanoparticles. Their localised surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) absorbs green ~525 nm; the complement is red. Particle shape and size shift the band: 10 nm spheres absorb 520 nm; 100 nm spheres widen and red-shift to ~600 nm; gold nanorods extend into the NIR.

Drag the particle size; the LSPR band moves and the solution colour changes from ruby red through violet to grey-brown.
